title: Velvety Spanish Bread Flatbread
description: A yeasty, flaky flatbread made with whole wheat and double rise for a lazy Sunday brunch.
instructions: In a large bowl combine whole wheat flour, all-purpose flour, salt, sugar, and instant yeast.
Warm the water to 110°F (43°C) and stir until dissolved.
Add the warm water, olive oil, and oregano to the dry ingredients and mix until a rough dough forms.
Knead the dough on a lightly floured surface for about 8 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Shape the dough into a ball, place in a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and let it rise for 1 hour or until doubled in size.
After the first rise, punch down the dough, roll it into a flat disc about 1/2 inch thick.
Place the flattened dough on a parchment-lined baking sheet and cover loosely with a clean towel.
Let the dough rise again for 30 minutes or until visibly puffy.
Preheat the oven to 425°F (220°C) and bake the flatbread for 12-15 minutes until golden and lightly crisp.
Allow the bread to cool for 5 minutes, slice, and serve warm.
